# tokens: # Mixed BarrierPos: 0 < B < N. Forward fills sig[0..B-1]; sig[B..N-1]
# must come from the stack. Distinct from /s (B=0) and /f (B=N) which
# are call-site overrides — here the boundary is intrinsic to the sig.
# Test word `pairq` has a single sig:
#   handler: (a, b) => formats `a:b` as a string
# So forward fills sig[0] (one token), sig[1] must come from the stack.

note: all-stack: sig[0]=top=5, sig[1]=2 (forward limit unused, stack supplies both)
all-forward: forward limit=1 fills sig[0]=5, sig[1] needs stack but stack empty
stack=[3,7], forward=[5]: sig[0]=5 (fwd), sig[1]=top=7 (stk); 3 left dangling
/f overrides barrier to N, both args from forward
/s overrides barrier to 0, only one stack value but two needed
